entries.title: Ecclesiastes — Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24-31. The scope of the whole is a comment on Pr 11:23. Thus liberality, by God's blessing, secures increase, while penuriousness, instead of expected gain, procures poverty.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 12 Pr 12:1-28. 1. loveth knowledge-- as the fruit of instruction or training (Pr 1:2). hateth reproof-- (Pr 10:17). brutish-- stupid, regardless of his own welfare (Psa 49:10; 73:22).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. Wickedness cannot give permanent prosperity. root ... not be moved-- firm as a flourishing tree--(Psa 1:3; 15:5; Jr 17:8).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. A virtuous woman-- in the wide sense of well-disposed to all moral duties (Pr 31:10). maketh ashamed-- that is, by misconduct. rottenness-- an incurable evil.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. thoughts-- or, "purposes." are right-- literally, "are judgment," that is, true decisions. counsels-- (Compare Pr 11:14). deceit-- contrary to truth and honesty.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. The words-- or, "expressed designs" of the wicked are for evil purposes. the mouth-- or, "words" of the righteous delivering instead of ensnaring men.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. Such conduct brings a proper return, by the destruction of the wicked and well-being of the righteous and his family.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. despised-- as opposed to commended (Pr 11:12). perverse heart-- or, "wicked principles," as opposed to one of wisdom.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. despised-- held in little repute, obscure (1Sa 18:23; Is 3:5). hath a servant-- implying some means of honest living. honoureth himself-- is self-conceited.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. regardeth-- literally, "knoweth" (Psa 1:6). mercies ... cruel-- as acts of compassion ungraciously rendered to the needy. The righteous more regards a beast than the wicked a man.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 1:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. The idler's fate is the result of indolence and want of principle (Pr 6:32; 7:7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. the wicked ... evil-- They love the crafty arts of deception. the root ... fruit-- their own resources supply them; or, it may be rendered: "He (God) giveth, or, sets (Eze 17:22) the root of the righteous," and hence it is firm: or, the verb is impersonal; "As to the root ... it is firm" (Pr 17:19).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:1–2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13-14. The wicked is snared, &c.-- The sentiment expanded. While the wicked, such as liars, flatterers, &c., fall by their own words, the righteous are unhurt. Their good conduct makes friends, and God rewards them.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. The way ... eyes-- The fool is self-conceited (compare Pr 12:1; 1:32; 10:17; Jas 3:17).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. prudent ... shame-- He is slow to denounce his insulters (Jas 1:19).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. speaketh-- literally, "speaketh hastily," or indiscreetly (Psa 106:33), as an angry man retorts harsh and provoking invectives. tongue ... health-- by soothing and gentle language.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. Words of truth are consistent, and stand all tests, while lies are soon discovered and exposed.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. that imagine-- or, "plan" (Pr 3:29). They design a deceitful course, to which, with all its evils and dangers to others and themselves, the happiness of peace-makers is opposed (compare Mt 5:9; Ro 12:18).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. no evil-- (as in Psa 91:10), under God's wise limitations (Ro 8:28). mischief-- as penal evil.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. deal truly-- or, "faithfully," that is, according to promises (compare Joh 3:21).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. concealeth-- by his modesty (Pr 10:14; 11:13). heart ... proclaimeth-- as his lips speak his thoughts (compare Ec 10:3).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. slothful-- (Compare Margin), so called because he fails to meet his promises. under tribute-- not denoting legal taxes, but the obligation of dependence.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. a good word-- one of comfort.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. more excellent-- (Compare Margin); or, "more successful," while the wicked fail; or, we may read it: "The righteous guides his friend, but," &c., that is, The ability of the righteous to aid others is contrasted with the ruin to which the way of the wicked leads themselves.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. (Compare Pr 12:24). took in hunting-- or, "his venison." He does not improve his advantages. the substance ... precious-- or, "the wealth of a man of honor is being diligent," or "diligence." precious-- literally, "honor" (Ec 10:1).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. (Compare Pr 8:8, 20, &c.). A sentiment often stated; here first affirmatively, then negatively.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:19
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. shall eat-- that is, obtain (Pr 12:14). transgressors-- as in Pr 2:22. violence-- or, "mischief" to themselves.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:20
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. He ... mouth ... life-- because evil speeches may provoke violence from others. he that openeth wide his lips shall have destruction-- On last clause, compare Pr 10:14.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:21
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:22
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. loathsome ... shame-- better, causeth shame and reproach (compare Pr 19:26), by slander, &c., which the righteous hates.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:23
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. A sentiment of frequent recurrence, that piety benefits and sin injures.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:24
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. In opposite ways men act hypocritically for gain of honor or wealth.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:25
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. Riches save some from punishment, while others suffer because they will not heed the rebuke of sloth, which makes and keeps them poor.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 2:26
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. light ... lamp-- prosperity; the first, the greater, and it rejoiceth-- burns brightly, or continues, while the other, at best small, soon fails.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. The obstinacy which attends self-conceit, produces contention, which the well-advised, thus evincing modesty, avoid.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. by vanity-- or, "nothingness," that is, which is vain or useless to the public (as card playing or similar vices). gathereth ... labour-- (Compare Margin), little by little, laboriously.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. desire cometh-- is realized. a tree of life-- or, "cause of happiness."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. the word-- that is, of advice, or, instruction (compare Pr 10:27; 11:31).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. (Compare Pr 10:11). fountain-- or, "source of life." to depart-- (compare Pr 1:2-4), or, "for departing," &c., and so gives life.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. Right perception and action secure good will, while evil ways are difficult as a stony road. The wicked left of God find punishment of sin in sinning. hard-- or, "harsh" (compare Hebrew: De 21:4; Jr 5:15).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. dealeth-- acts with foresight. a fool ... folly-- for want of caution.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. A wicked-- or, "unfaithful" messenger falleth into mischief-- or, "by mischief," or "evil," and so his errand fails. Contrasted is the character of the faithful, whose faithfulness benefits others.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. Self-denial, which fools will not endure, is essential to success.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. The benefits of good and evil of bad society are contrasted.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. (Compare Pr 11:31). good ... repaid-- or, "He (God) will repay good."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. wealth ... just-- While good men's estates remain in their families, God so orders that the gains of sinners enure to the just (compare Pr 28:8; Psa 37:18, 22, 26, &c.).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. The laboring poor prosper more than those who injudiciously or wickedly strive, by fraud and violence, to supersede the necessity of lawful labor.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. spareth-- or, "withholds." rod-- of correction. hateth-- or, acts as if he hated him (compare Pr 3:12; 8:36). chasteneth ... betimes-- or, "diligently seeks for him all useful discipline."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. The comparative temporal prosperity of the righteous and wicked, rather than contentment and discontent, is noted.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 14 Pr 14:1-35. 1. Every wise, &c.-- literally, "The wisdoms" (compare Pr 9:1) "of women," plural, a distributive form of speech. buildeth ... house-- increases wealth, which the foolish, by mismanagement, lessen.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. uprightness-- is the fruit of fearing God, as falsehood and ill-nature (Pr 2:15; 3:32) of despising Him and His law.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:19
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. rod of pride-- that is, the punishment of pride, which they evince by their words. The words of the wise procure good to them.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:20
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. crib is clean-- empty; so "cleanness of teeth" denotes want of food (compare Am 4:6). Men get the proper fruit of their doings (Ga 6:7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:21
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. A faithful witness, &c.-- one tested to be such. utter lies-- or, "breathe out lies"--that is, habitually lies (Pr 6:19; compare Ac 9:1). Or the sense is, that habitual truthfulness, or lying, will be evinced in witness-bearing.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 3:22
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. An humble, teachable spirit succeeds in seeking (Pr 8:9; Joh 7:17; Jas 1:5, 6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. Avoid the society of those who cannot teach you.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. Appearances deceive the thoughtless, but the prudent discriminate.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. Fools make a mock at sin-- or, "Sin deludes fools." righteous ... favour-- that is, of God, instead of the punishment of sin.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. Each one best knows his own sorrows or joys.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. (Compare Pr 12:7). The contrast of the whole is enhanced by that of house and tabernacle, a permanent and a temporary dwelling.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. end thereof-- or, "reward," what results (compare Pr 5:4). ways of death-- leading to it.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. The preceding sentiment illustrated by the disappointments of a wicked or untimely joy.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. filled ... ways-- receive retribution (Pr 1:31). a good man ... himself-- literally, "is away from such," will not associate with him.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. The simple ... word-- He is credulous, not from love, but heedlessness (Pr 13:16).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. (Compare Pr 3:7; 28:14). rageth-- acts proudly and conceitedly.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. He ... angry-- literally, "short of anger" (compare Pr 14:29, opposite idea). man ... hated-- that is, the deliberate evildoer is more hated than the rash.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. inherit-- as a portion (compare Pr 3:35). are crowned-- literally, "are surrounded with it," abound in it.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. Describes the humbling of the wicked by the punishment their sins incur.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. This sad but true picture of human nature is not given approvingly, but only as a fact.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. For such contempt of the poor is contrasted as sinful with the virtuous compassion of the good.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. As usual, the interrogative negative strengthens the affirmative. mercy and truth-- that is, God's (Psa 57:3; 61:7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 4:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. labour-- painful diligence. talk ... penury-- idle and vain promises and plans.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. (Compare Pr 3:16). foolishness ... folly-- Folly remains, or produces folly; it has no benefit.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. Life often depends on truth-telling. a deceitful ... lies-- He that breathes out lies is deceit, not to be trusted (Pr 14:5).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. The blessings of piety descend to children (Pr 13:22; 20:7; Ex 20:6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. (Compare Pr 13:14). fear of the Lord-- or, "law of the wise," is wisdom (Psa 111:10).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. The teaching of a true political economy.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. slow ... understanding-- (Compare Pr 14:17). hasty-- (Compare Pr 14:17). exalteth folly-- makes it conspicuous, as if delighting to honor it.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 30. A sound heart-- both literally and figuratively, a source of health; in the latter sense, opposed to the known effect of evil passions on health.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 31. reproacheth his Maker-- who is the God of such, as well as of the rich (Pr 22:2; Job 31:15; and specially 1Sa 2:8; Psa 113:7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 32. driven-- thrust out violently (compare Psa 35:5, 6). hath hope-- trusteth (Pr 10:2; 11:4; Psa 2:12), implying assurance of help.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 33. resteth-- preserved in quietness for use, while fools blazon their folly (Pr 12:23; 13:16).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 34. Righteousness-- just principles and actions. exalteth-- raises to honor. is a reproach-- brings on them the ill-will of others (compare Pr 13:6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 35. wise-- discreet or prudent. causeth shame-- (Pr 10:5; 12:4) acts basely.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 15 Pr 15:1-33. 1. soft-- tender or gentle. turneth ... wrath-- from any one. stir up-- as a smouldering fire is excited.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. useth ... aright-- commends knowledge by its proper use. poureth out-- utters abundantly (Pr 12:23), and so disgusts others.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. beholding-- watching (compare Pr 5:21; Psa 66:7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. A wholesome tongue-- (Compare Margin), pacifying and soothing language. tree of life-- (Pr 3:18; 11:30). perverseness therein-- cross, ill-natured language. breach ... spirit-- (compare Is 65:14, Hebrew), grieves, instead of appeasing.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. (Compare Pr 4:1; 10:17; 13:1-18). is prudent-- acts discreetly.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. treasure-- implying utility. trouble-- vexation and affliction.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:19
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. (Compare Pr 10:20, 21). heart ... not so-- not right, or vain.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 5:20–6:0
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8-9. The sacrifice [and] prayer-- are acts of worship. way ... followeth ... righteousness-- denote conduct. God's regard for the worship and deeds of the righteous and wicked respectively, so stated in Psa 50:17; Is 1:11.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. (Compare Pr 10:17). the way-- that in which God would have him to go (Pr 2:13; Psa 119:1).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. Hell-- (Psa 16:10). destruction-- or, "Abaddon," the place of the destroyer. All the unseen world is open to God, much more men's hearts.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. (Compare Pr 9:8). go unto the wise-- to be instructed.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. maketh ... countenance-- or, "benefits the countenance." spirit is broken-- and so the countenance is sad.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. (Compare Pr 10:21, 22). The wise grow wiser, the fools more foolish (Pr 9:9).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. The state of the heart governs the outward condition. evil-- sad, contrasted with the cheerfulness of a feast.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. trouble-- agitation, implying the anxieties and perplexities attending wealth held by worldlings (Pr 16:18; 1Ti 6:6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. dinner-- or, "allowance" (2Ki 25:30)-- of herbs-- and that the plainest. and hatred-- (compare Pr 10:12, 18).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. The difficulties of the slothful result from want of energy; the righteous find a plain [and open] way-- literally, "a highway," by diligence (1Sa 10:7; Psa 1:3).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. (Compare Pr 10:1).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 6:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. walketh uprightly-- and so finds his joy (Pr 3:6; 10:23).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. Without counsel-- or, "deliberation," implying a wise deference to the opinions of the wise and good, contrasted with rashness.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. Good advice blesses the giver and receiver.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. (Compare Col 3:2). Holy purposes prevent sinning, and so its evils.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. The most desolate who have God's aid have more permanent good than the self-reliant sinner (Pr 2:22; 12:7). border-- or, "boundary for possessions" (Psa 78:54).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. are pleasant words-- that is, pleasing to God (Pr 8:8, 9).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. (Compare Pr 11:17). Avarice brings trouble to him and his. hateth gifts-- or, "bribes" (Ex 23:8; Psa 15:5), and is not avaricious.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. (Compare Pr 15:14; 10:11). Caution is the fruit of wisdom; rashness of folly.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. far ... wicked-- in His love and favor (Psa 22:11; 119:155).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 30. light of the eyes-- (Pr 13:9). What gives light rejoiceth the heart, by relieving from anxiety as to our course; so good report-- or, "doctrine" (Is 28:9; 53:1), maketh ... fat-- or, "gives prosperity" (Pr 3:13-17; 9:11). The last clause is illustrated by the first.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:9–10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 31-32. (Compare Pr 10:17). reproof of life-- which leads to life. abideth ... wise-- is numbered among them.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 33. The fear ... wisdom-- Wisdom instructs in true piety. before ... humility-- (compare Lu 24:26; 1Pe 1:11); opposite (compare Pr 16:18).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 16 Pr 16:1-33. 1. preparations-- schemes. in man-- or literally, "to man," belonging, or pertaining to him. the answer ... Lord-- The efficient ordering is from God: "Man proposes; God disposes."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. clean-- or, "faultless." weigheth-- or, "tries," "judges," implying that they are faulty (Pr 21:2; 24:12).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. (Compare Margin). Rely on God for success to your lawful purposes.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. for himself-- "for its answer," or "purpose," that is, according to God's plan; the wicked are for the day of evil (Psa 49:5; Jr 17:18); sinning and suffering answer to each other, are indissolubly united.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. (Compare Pr 3:32).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. By mercy and truth-- that is, God's (Psa 85:10); He effects the atonement, or covering of sin; and the principles of true piety incline men to depart from evil; or, "mercy" and "truth" may be man's, indicative of the gracious tempers which work instrumentally in procuring pardon. purged-- expiated (as in Le 16:33; Is 27:9, Hebrew).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:19
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. Persecutions, of course, excepted.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:20
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:21
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. (Compare Pr 16:3). directeth-- establisheth.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:22
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. The last clause depends on the first, expressing the importance of equity in decisions, so authoritative.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:23
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. are the Lord's ... his work-- that is, what He has ordered, and hence should be observed by men.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:24
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. Rulers are rightly expected, by their position, to hate evil; for their power is sustained by righteousness.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:25
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. A specification of the general sentiment of Pr 16:12.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:26
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. This wrath, so terrible and certain, like messengers of death (1Ki 2:25), can be appeased by the wise.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:27
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. light of ... countenance-- favor (Psa 4:6). life-- preserves it, or gives blessings which make it valuable. the latter rain-- fell just before harvest and matured the crop; hence specially valuable (De 11:14).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:28
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 7:29
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. The highway-- A common, plain road represents the habitual course of the righteous in departing from evil. keepeth-- observes.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18-19. (Compare Pr 15:33). Haughtiness and pride imply self-confidence which produces carelessness, and hence a fall-- literally, "sliding."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. handleth a matter-- wisely considers "the word," that is, of God (compare Pr 13:13). trusteth-- (Compare Psa 2:12; 118:8, 9).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. wise in heart-- who rightly consider duty. sweetness of the lips-- eloquent discourse, persuades and instructs others.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. Understanding-- or, "discretion," is a constant source of blessing (Pr 13:14), benefiting others; but fools' best efforts are folly.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. The heart is the source of wisdom flowing from the mouth.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. (Compare Pr 15:26). Gentle, kind words, by soothing the mind, give the body health.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. (Compare Pr 14:2).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. Diligence is a duty due to one's self, for his wants require labor.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27. ungodly man-- (Compare Pr 6:12). diggeth up evil-- labors for it. in his lips ... fire-- His words are calumniating (Jas 3:6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 28. (Compare Pr 6:14; 10:31). whisperer-- prater, talebearer (Pr 18:8; 26:20).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 29. violent man-- or, "man of mischief" (Pr 3:31). enticeth-- (Pr 1:10).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 30. He shutteth his eyes-- denoting deep thought (Psa 64:6). moving his lips-- or, "biting his lips"--a determined purpose (Pr 6:13).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 31. (Compare Pr 20:29). if-- or, which may be supplied properly, or without it the sense is as in Pr 3:16; 4:10, that piety is blessed with long life.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 32. (Compare Pr 14:29). taketh a city-- that is, by fighting.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 33. Seemingly the most fortuitous events are ordered by God.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 8:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 17 Pr 17:1-28. 1. sacrifices-- or, "feasts" made with part of them (compare Pr 7:14; Le 2:3; 7:31). with-- literally, "of." strife-- its product, or attendant.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. (Compare Pr 14:35). causeth shame-- (Pr 10:5). shall ... inheritance-- that is, share a brother's part (compare Nu 27:4, 7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. God only knows, as He tries (Psa 12:6; 66:10) the heart.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. Wicked doers and speakers alike delight in calumny.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. (Compare Pr 14:31). glad at calamities-- rejoicing in others' evil. Such are rightly punished by God, who knows their hearts.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6. Prolonged posterity is a blessing, its cutting off a curse (Pr 13:22; Psa 109:13-15), hence children may glory in virtuous ancestry.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 7. Excellent speech-- (Compare Margin). Such language as ill suits a fool, as lying (ought to suit) a prince (Pr 16:12, 13).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. One so corrupt as to take a bribe evinces his high estimate of it by subjection to its influence (Pr 18:16; 19:6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. seeketh love-- (Compare Margin). The contrast is between the peace-maker and tale-bearer.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. Reproof more affects the wise than severe scourging, fools.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. Such meet just retribution (1Ki 2:25). a cruel messenger-- one to inflict it.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. They are less rational in anger than wild beasts.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. (Compare Psa 7:4; 35:12). evil-- injury to another (Pr 13:21).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. letteth ... water-- as a breach in a dam. before ... meddled with-- before strife has become sharp, or, by an explanation better suiting the figure, before it rolls on, or increases.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 15. abomination ... Lord-- as reversing His method of acting (Pr 3:32; 12:2).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. Though wealth cannot buy wisdom for those who do not love it, yet wisdom procures wealth (Pr 3:16; 14:24).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. To the second of these parallel clauses, there is an accession of meaning, that is, that a brother's love is specially seen in adversity.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. (Compare Pr 6:1-5; 11:15). in the presence, &c.-- that is, he either fails to consult his friend, or to follow his advice.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. strife-- contention is, and leads to, sin. he that exalteth his gate-- gratifies a vain love of costly building. seeketh-- or, "findeth," as if he sought (compare "loveth death," Pr 8:36).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 9:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. The second clause advances on the first. The ill-natured fail of good, and the cavilling and fault-finding incur evil.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. (Compare Pr 23:24). Different words are rendered by "fool," both denoting stupidity and impiety.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. (Compare Pr 14:30; 15:13). The effect of the mind on the body is well known. medicine-- or, "body," which better corresponds with "bone." drieth-- as if the marrow were exhausted.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. a gift ... bosom-- Money and other valuables were borne in a fold of the garment, called the bosom. to pervert-- that is, by bribery.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. Wisdom ... him-- ever an object of regard, while a fool's affections are unsettled.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 25. a grief-- or cross, vexation (compare Pr 17:21; 10:1).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 26. Also-- that is, Equally to be avoided are other sins: punishing good subjects, or resisting good rulers.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:6–7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 27-28. Prudence of speech is commended as is an excellent or calm spirit, not excited to vain conversation.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 18 Pr 18:1-24. 1. Through desire ... seeketh-- that is, seeks selfish gratification. intermeddleth ... wisdom-- or, "rushes on" (Pr 17:14) against all wisdom, or what is valuable (Pr 2:7).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. that his heart ... itself-- that is, takes pleasure in revealing his folly (Pr 12:23; 15:2).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. So surely are sin and punishment connected (Pr 16:4). wicked, for "wickedness," answers to ignominy, or the state of such; and contempt, the feeling of others to them; and to reproach, a manifestation of contempt.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. Wise speech is like an exhaustless stream of benefit.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. accept the person-- (Compare Psa 82:2). "It is not good" is to be supplied before "to overthrow."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:14–15
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 6-7. The quarrelsome bring trouble on themselves. Their rash language ensnares them (Pr 6:2).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:16
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. (Compare Pr 16:28). as wounds-- not sustained by the Hebrew; better, as "sweet morsels," which men gladly swallow. innermost ... belly-- the mind, or heart (compare Pr 20:27-30; Psa 22:14).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:17
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 9. One by failing to get, the other by wasting wealth, grows poor. waster-- literally, "master of washing," a prodigal.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:18
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. name of the Lord-- manifested perfections (Psa 8:1; 20:2), as faithfulness, power, mercy, &c., on which men rely. is safe-- literally, "set on high, out of danger" (Psa 18:2; 91:4).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:19
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. contrasts with Pr 18:10 (compare Pr 10:15). Such is a vain trust (compare Psa 73:6).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 10:20
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. Hasty speech evinces self-conceit, and ensures shame (Pr 26:12).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:1
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 14. infirmity-- bodily sickness, or outward evil. The spirit, which sustains, being wounded, no support is left, except, as implied, in God.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 16. (Compare Pr 17:8, 23). Disapproval of the fact stated is implied.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 17. One-sided statements are not reliable. searcheth-- thoroughly (Pr 17:9, 19).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 18. The lot-- whose disposal is of God (Pr 16:13), may, properly used, be a right mode of settling disputes.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 19. No feuds so difficult of adjustment as those of relatives; hence great care should be used to avoid them.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:7
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 20. (Compare Pr 12:14; 13:2). Men's words are the fruit, or, increase of his lips, and when good, benefit them. satisfied with-- (Compare Pr 1:31; 14:14).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:8
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 21. Death and life-- or, the greatest evil and good. that love it-- that is, the tongue, or its use for good or evil. eat ... fruit-- (Compare Pr 18:19; Jas 1:19).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 22. The old versions supply "good" before the "wife," as the last clause and Pr 19:14 imply (compare Pr 31:10).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 11:10
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 23. the rich ... roughly-- He is tolerated because rich, implying that the estimate of men by wealth is wrong.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12 — Chapter Introduction
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 24. A man ... friendly-- better, "A man ... (is) to, or, may triumph (Psa 108:9), or, shout for joy (Psa 5:11), that is, may congratulate himself." Indeed, there is a Friend who is better than a brother; such is the "Friend of sinners" [Mt 11:19; Lu 7:34], who may have been before the writer's mind.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:2
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: CHAPTER 19 Pr 19:1-29. 1. (Compare Pr 28:6). "Rich" for fool here. Integrity is better than riches (Pr 15:16, 17; 16:8).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:3
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 2. The last illustrates the first clause. Rashness, the result of ignorance, brings trouble.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:4
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 3. perverteth ... way-- turns him back from right (Pr 13:6; Jas 1:13); and he blames God for his failures.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:5
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 4. (Compare Pr 14:20). Such facts are often adduced with implied disapprobation.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:6
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 5. Compare Pr 19:9, where perish explains not escape here (compare Psa 88:9, 10).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:9
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 8. (Compare Margin; Pr 15:32). loveth ... soul-- or, "himself," which he evinces by regarding his best interests. keepeth-- or, "regards."

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:11
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 10. (Compare Pr 17:7). The fool is incapable of properly using pleasure as knowledge, yet for him to have it is less incongruous than the undue elevation of servants. Let each abide in his calling (1Co 7:20).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:12
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 11. (Compare Pr 14:29; 16:32). This inculcation of a forgiving spirit shows that true religion is always the same (Mt 5:22-24).

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:13
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 12. (Compare Pr 16:14, 15; 20:2). A motive to submission to lawful authority.

entries.title: Ecclesiastes 12:14
entries.author: Robert Jamieson, A. R. Fausset, and David Brown
entries.content: 13. calamity-- literally, "calamities," varied and many. continual dropping-- a perpetual annoyance, wearing out patience.
